transcript.title: Opening
transcript.text: Good morning Rushil Bhatia. Here is your daily news for Thursday, July 2. In today's briefing: top headlines, bhopal and madhya pradesh, business and markets, money, tax and personal finance, science and technology, development and policy, across india, around the world. Let's begin.

transcript.title: Top headlines
transcript.text: First, the stories leading the news this morning. Farooq, Mehbooba join call for India-Pak dialogue, BJP says terror and talks can't go together. Former J and K CMs Farooq Abdullah and Mehbooba Mufti, alongside nearly 60 Indian signatories, have urged India and Pakistan to resume dialogue for regional peace. Their letter highlights the lost opportunities for millions of young people due to ongoing hostility. Kashmir's chief cleric also supported the call, emphasizing that engagement is the only way to resolve differences and build a prosperous South Asia. Empire State Building climb: Couple arrested after proposal atop NYC's skyscraper - video. A daring stunt saw two individuals scale the Empire State Building without safety gear, unfurling a peace banner and reportedly getting engaged at the summit. Ivan Kuznetsov and Angelina Nikolau, known for such climbs, were apprehended after accessing the spire via a maintenance hatch. They face multiple charges including burglary and reckless endangerment for their audacious act. Rain washes out India-England T20I opener after Iyer, Abhishek power India to 189/7. India's T20I opener against England was washed out due to persistent rain, preventing a chase after the hosts posted a formidable 189/7. Abhishek Sharma's blistering 59 and Shreyas Iyer's composed 68 laid the foundation, with Shivam Dube adding crucial late runs. Unfortunately, the weather had the final say, leaving the match a no-result without a ball bowled in England's innings. US Seahawk chopper makes emergency water landing in Arabian Sea; one crew member missing. A US Navy Sea Hawk helicopter made an emergency water landing in the Arabian Sea early Tuesday, with one crew member still missing. Three of the four crew members were rescued and are in stable condition. The US Navy is conducting search operations for the missing individual. The cause of the incident is under investigation, with no indication of hostile action amid regional tensions. Days after Meta Layoffs, Zuckerberg says companies should empower workers, not replace them. Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg believes AI-driven job losses are exaggerated, advocating for tools that boost individual productivity. This comes as the tech giant laid off 8,000 employees, reassigning another 7,000 to AI projects, to offset significant investments in its AI "reboot." While Meta pours billions into AI infrastructure, Zuckerberg suggests a focus on human empowerment could create more jobs, a view contrasting with some industry leaders. Board weightage mooted in entrance exam revamp. Following the NEET-UG controversy, the Prime Minister's Office is now overseeing significant exam reforms. The Education Ministry is fast-tracking changes for board exams and entrance tests like NEET and JEE, aiming to reduce the pressure of single-attempt exams. Proposals include incorporating board marks, aligning entrance tests with school syllabi, and exploring multiple attempts and computer-based testing to enhance integrity and minimize risks. 'Indian-American endorsing Pakistani-American': Ro Khanna backs candidate Aisha Farooqi. In a heartfelt endorsement, US Congressman Ro Khanna rallied behind Aisha Farooqi, a Pakistani American candidate vying for Michigan's 11th Congressional District. Their conversation, shared through video, celebrated South Asian heritage and America's rich tapestry of diversity, even venturing into discussions about Bollywood, food, and sports. Khanna proudly represents California's 17th, emphasizing the power of personal connections in politics. Sergey Brin sold his NYC real estate: Here's what Mamdani's rent freeze cost him. Google co-founder Sergey Brin has divested his stake in a New York City real estate fund for a fraction of its value, selling for just six cents on the dollar. This significant loss comes as Mayor Zohran Mamdani's rent freeze tightens conditions for landlords. The fund, A and E Real Estate, confirmed the sale, citing the challenging market for rent-stabilized apartments. ATF, commercial LPG prices cut as global crude cost eases. Commercial LPG and aviation fuel prices have seen a significant drop, with state-run companies cutting prices by rupees 183.5 and rupees 5 respectively. This easing follows a decline in global crude prices and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. However, domestic LPG and petrol/diesel prices remain unchanged, as oil companies continue to absorb losses from earlier elevated crude bookings. Airfare relief is also unlikely due to ongoing airspace issues. Government asks WhatsApp to pause username rollout. NEW DELHI: Government has directed Meta-owned WhatsApp not to roll out its proposed usernames feature in India until further consultations are completed, while asking the company to submit a detailed explanation within three days on how the system will operate and prevent misuse. UD ministry sends eviction notice to Gymkhana Club. The government has issued an eviction notice to the Delhi Gymkhana Club, citing its "unauthorised occupation of public premises" after the land lease expired on May 22. The Land and Development Office is seeking an explanation for why eviction proceedings shouldn't commence, invoking the Public Premises Act. The 27.3-acre land is deemed critically needed for defence, public security, and other public-interest projects.

transcript.title: Bhopal and Madhya Pradesh
transcript.text: Now, news closer to home in Bhopal and across Madhya Pradesh. Bhopal's Wait For Master Plan To Be Lengthy, UAD To Make It Afresh. The BJP government issued a draft Master Plan in 2023, which the Mohan Yadav-led government cancelled. Congress High Command Angry With Digvijaya For Second Time. Singh has erred again, and after the cancellation of Meenakshi Natarajan's nomination form for the Rajya Sabha election, Rahul has gone at the MP government hammer and tongs. Six From MP Among 8 Killed In Bus Inferno; 13 Injured, Two Still Missing. Eyewitnesses alleged that ambulances and fire tenders reached the spot nearly an hour after the blaze erupted despite police and expressway authorities being alerted around 3.20 am. Stamp Duty: Penalty Imposed On Firm Linked To Sanjay Pathak's Family. The findings state that during the registration of four of the five land parcels, stamp duty amounting to rupees 71.66 lakh and registration fees of more than rupees 6 lakh were allegedly evaded. 56 Economically Weaker Section Houses Booked In 20 Mins; Booking Process Comes Under Probe. Sources also claimed that a significant number of successful bookings originated from private bank accounts linked to an Indore branch, raising further suspicions. IMD warns of extremely heavy rainfall in several Madhya Pradesh districts. Arrest warrant against MP Cong chief over 2024 poll speech. Indore Municipal Corporation Imposes rupees 1.25 Lakh Fine In Crackdown On Banned Plastic. The confiscated material is being transported to NEPRA for scientific and environmentally safe disposal in accordance with prescribed norms. Daily Madhya Pradesh Current Affairs for MPPSC Exam (02-07-2026). Madhya Pradesh's Terror Trail And The Aadhaar Alarm BSNL Never Escalated. Arun Yadav urges Rahul Gandhi to step in as Digvijaya Singh-Jitu Patwari row exposes MP Congress rift. India grants GI status to 12 horticultural products from Madhya Pradesh. Madhya Pradesh High Court calls in experts over rape accused's 'deaf and mute' claim. Docs must give definite opinion in autopsy reports, says MP HC. Man rapes, blackmails woman for four months. Madhya Pradesh's business-friendly policies are unmatched: SAGE Group Chairman Sanjeev Agrawal. BMC auctions fire station access, emergency response to be stuck in lanes in rains. Three duped in separate cyber fraud cases.

transcript.title: Business and markets
transcript.text: Moving to business, the economy and the markets. Gold, silver prices today: Comex gains as softer US jobs data, Warsh remarks ease Fed hike bets. Gold and silver prices rose on July 1 after a poor quarterly performance, aided by easing fears of a US rate hike and a weaker dollar. Despite recent volatility, markets are pricing a 65 percent chance of a September rate hike as employment data falls short of expectations. Meta Platforms surges 12 percent on report of AI cloud service plans to rival Amazon, Microsoft. Meta Platforms' shares rose nearly 12 percent after reports of launching a cloud computing business aimed at selling AI services. The initiative, part of Meta Compute, could position the company against Amazon and Microsoft in the AI infrastructure market. Adani Green Energy to announce June quarter results on this date. Check details. Adani Green Energy's Board will meet on July 22, 2026, to approve financial results for Q1FY27. For the March ending quarter, the company reported a 34 percent year-on-year (YoY) increase in consolidated net profit to rupees 514 crore Indian Bank Q1 business update: Lender reports 13.3 percent growth in deposits, advances rise 13.9 percent. Indian Bank reported a 13.6 percent YoY growth in total business to rupees 15.28 lakh crore for Q1 FY26. Deposits rose 13.3 percent and gross advances increased 13.9 percent . Net profit for the quarter reached rupees 3,103 crore, while asset quality improved with GNPA and NNPA ratios declining. Multibagger Paisalo Digital hits 20 percent upper circuit as promoters raise stake to 46.72 percent. Paisalo Digital's shares hit a 20 percent upper circuit at rupees 71 after promoters increased their stake to 46.72 percent . This marks a steady rise in shareholding from 26 percent in FY19, showcasing long-term confidence and a strategy for doubling assets, income, and profit within three years. Gold price drops below $4,000, down 12 percent in June - biggest monthly decline since 2008: what lies ahead?. Gold has fallen below the critical $4,000 level, sliding to around $3,960. Analysts predict potential rebounds due to oversold conditions, while long-term fundamentals remain supportive despite ongoing volatility tied to US interest rates and geopolitical factors. Top Gainers and losers on 1 July: Reliance Power, Delhivery, Eternal, Paytm, MCX, Dabur India among top gainers. The Indian stock market opened positively in July, led by strong buying in realty, FMCG, and auto stocks, with Nifty 50 at 24,005 and Sensex at 76,873. However, IT and metal sectors saw declines as geopolitical tensions impacted crude oil prices. Sensex rises over 400 points, Nifty 50 ends above 24,000; investors earn over rupees 2 lakh crore in a day. Equity benchmark Sensex clocked a gain of 444 points, or 0.58 percent , to close at 76,922.64, while the Nifty 50 settled 140 points, or 0.59 percent , higher at 24,005.85. In Graphics: India's wealthy cement their seat. While the USA remains the primary millionaire factory, India outpaced many developed markets in growth. Japanese Yen sinks to weakest level since 1986: 3 reasons behind the historic slide. The yen has reached its lowest point against the US dollar in almost 40 years, prompting worries of intervention from Japanese authorities. The decline is fueled by a strong dollar, expectations of US rate hikes, and growing bearish bets on the yen, despite Japan's recent economic improvements. Bata to refund student over rupees 67 shoe overcharge. A law student's rupees 67 overcharge on shoes led to a significant ruling against Bata India by the Kerala Consumer Commission. Aurobindo Pharma's investors await next leg of growth. Growth would be aided by the Pen-G project ramp-up, Lannett's integration, new US product launches, expansion in Europe and contribution from higher-margin businesses Iran insists on keeping control over Hormuz. Iran is determined to gain international recognition for its control over the Strait of Hormuz, including the right to levy fees on ships. Infosys shares slip below rupees 1,000 for first time since 2020 as IT rout deepens; m-cap falls to rupees 4 lakh crore. Infosys shares have dropped 40 percent this year, falling below rupees 1,000 for the first time since September 2020. Contributing factors include AI disruption fears, weak earnings, and rising interest rates. Bangladesh sends mangoes to WB and Tripura CMs. Bangladesh has extended a sweet gesture to India, dispatching 1,100 kgs of Himsagar and Amrapali mangoes to West Bengal Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari and Tripura Chief Minister Manik Saha. Expert view: A smooth rally unlikely in short term; markets not out of macro woods, says Rishabh Nahar of Qode Advisors. Expert view: Rishabh Nahar from Qode Advisors offers a cautious outlook on the Indian stock market's future amid geopolitical tensions and economic factors. He advocates for a gradual, earnings-led approach rather than expecting a smooth rally. Harsh Goenka shares simple formula for growth. Industrialist Harsh Goenka's "3-30-300 Rule of Life" highlights the profound impact of relationships on personal and professional growth. Advit Jewels share price hits 5 percent lower circuit after bumper debut. Should you buy, sell or hold?. Advit Jewels shares surged on debut, listing at rupees 187 on BSE and rupees 188.90 on NSE, marking gains over the IPO price of rupees 138. Despite hitting a 5 percent lower circuit, shares remain above IPO price by 30.04 percent on NSE and 28.73 percent on BSE. FIFA World Cup 2026 today match schedule. FIFA World Cup 2026 match schedule for 2 July: Football fever grips India as the FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 32 heats up on July 2. CSM Technologies IPO listing tomorrow. Here's what GMP signals ahead of debut. CSM Technologies IPO was open for subscription on June 24 and closed on June 29. Government begins hunt for a new ONGC chairman. The government is actively seeking a new chairman for ONGC, with applications now open. India sits out the global bull run in the first half of 2026. Indian equities logged their worst first-half performance since 2022, with the Nifty falling 8.7 percent as war-driven volatility, record foreign outflows and an AI-led IT sector rout weighed on sentiment. Domestic institutional buying of rupees 4.63 trillion cushioned the blow. rupees 15K monthly investment for 20 years? NPS or MF. Retirement planning often pits National Pension System (NPS) against equity mutual funds. Manufacturing PMI: Inflation pressure is receding, so is growth. Moderating crude prices, if sustained, is a relief for overall inflation and even for manufacturers. However, slow progress of the southwest monsoon could emerge as a demand headwind US, Iran enter technical talks to secure peace deal. Technical discussions between the U.S. Samir Arora of Helios Capital backs SIPs, challenges critics: 'Prove market timing works'. SIPs are popular among retail investors for building wealth, but debates on market timing arise during volatility. Fund manager Samir Arora challenges SIP critics to prove that timing works, suggesting they demonstrate successful examples of such strategies instead. Trump upbeat as US, Iran hold indirect talks. US President Donald Trump hailed the progress on Wednesday of indirect talks between the United States and Iran in Qatar, as the sides aimed to push forward negotiations and quell tensions following exchanges of fire.

transcript.title: Money, tax and personal finance
transcript.text: Now to your money — taxes, savings and personal finance. Losing airport lounge access on your credit card? Paisabazaar launches PB Pass for Indian, global lounge entry. Paisabazaar has launched PB Pass, a new complimentary airport lounge access program for its credit card users, offering entry to over 1,800 lounges globally. This benefit is available to new credit card applicants upon activation and first bill payment via the Paisabazaar app. HDFC Bank credit card caps SmartBuy brand voucher rewards from July 1 , What changes for customers?. HDFC Bank has revised its SmartBuy rewards program, introducing a cap on reward points for brand voucher purchases on major premium credit cards. Here's what changes for customers. EPF Scheme 2026 replaces EPF Scheme 1952: Have EPF interest rate, contribution and wage ceiling changed?. EPF Scheme 2026: The Employees' Provident Funds Scheme, 2026, has replaced the 1952 version, bringing the EPF under the new Social Security Code. While key benefits like contribution rates and UAN remain unchanged, the new framework emphasizes digital processes and introduces stricter governance for exempted trusts. Buying the dip versus SIP: Which strategy delivers better returns? Here's what math says. Market swings often lead investors to seek ideal buying moments. However, historical data shows systematic investment plans outperform market timing strategies, as cash on the sidelines incurs opportunity costs. How a Multi Asset Allocation brings equity, debt and gold together in one place. Diversification is key to successful investing, and Multi-Asset Allocation Funds offer a balanced approach. These funds invest in at least three asset classes, including equity for growth, debt for stability, and gold for diversification, ensuring a resilient portfolio. By combining these elements, investors gain exposure to multiple return sources through a single, convenient solution, helping navigate market cycles and achieve long-term goals. EPF Scheme 2026 notified under Social Security Code: New withdrawal rules and other changes you should know. The Centre has implemented the EPF Scheme, 2026, replacing the 1952 scheme. Effective June 29, 2026, it retains contribution rates while enhancing digital compliance and simplifying withdrawal processes for members. Details here. rupees 15,000 monthly investment for 20 years: NPS or mutual funds? Know which may leave you with a bigger retirement corpus. Retirement planning often pits National Pension System (NPS) against equity mutual funds. While mutual funds offer flexibility and potentially higher returns, NPS boasts significant tax advantages and low costs. Read the article to know how a monthly investment of rupees 15,000, which goes up by 5 percent every year, can grow over 20 years in NPS and mutual funds. SK Hynix heads to Nasdaq: Should Indian investors choose it over Micron? Which is the better long-term bet?. SK Hynix commands a significant share of the HBM market and its Nasdaq listing may raise billions for expansion. However, the overall demand is high, suggesting both SK Hynix and Micron can grow together despite increased competition in memory chips. Investing for your child's future through PPF? Here's what every parent should know before opening a PPF account. Parents can open a Public Provident Fund (PPF) account for their minor children to secure their future, benefiting from compounding over the 15-year maturity period. While the annual contribution limit is rupees 1.5 lakh across all minor accounts and the parent's own PPF, it offers tax benefits under the old regime. Dream Money to shut ops on July 30 within a year of launch: What happens to your mutual funds, FDs and digital gold. Dream Sports will shut down its fintech platform Dream Money on July 30. Here's what happens to your mutual funds, fixed deposits and digital gold. 22k gold rate today: Check 24k, 22k, 20k, 18k gold prices (July 1, 2026) at IBJA, Malabar Gold and Diamonds, Tanishq, Joyalukkas and Kalyan Jewellers. Gold prices saw a slight dip today, July 1, 2026, across major Indian jewellers. Consumers looking to purchase gold jewellery will find it beneficial to compare rates from brands like Tanishq, Malabar Gold and Diamonds, Joyalukkas, and Kalyan Jewellers. Selling property or stocks? These 5 smart hacks can help you save capital gains tax. Capital gains tax can be minimized through strategic planning and compliance with the Income Tax Act. Taxpayers may claim exemptions, utilize capital losses, consider holding periods, maintain accurate records, and time sales effectively to reduce their tax liability legally. EPS 2026 replaces EPS-71, EPS-95: What has changed in new EPFO pension scheme and what remains the same. A new Employees' Pension Scheme, 2026, has replaced earlier pension schemes, effective June 29, 2026. While core benefits like the pension formula and contributions remain, key changes include faster pension claim settlements within 20 days and a 12 percent interest penalty for EPFO delays. FCNR deposits are a 'no-brainer' for NRIs, so what may be holding them back? Zerodha's Nithin Kamath explains. Zerodha's Nithin Kamath highlights long account-opening processes as a barrier for NRIs to benefit from FCNR deposits. The RBI's favorable changes make it easier, but slow onboarding could diminish interest. Can central government employees get 3 percent or 4 percent DA hike in July 2026? Latest AICPI data reveals this. Labour Bureau reading for DA hike: The latest AICPI-IW data for May 2026 indicates that central government employees are likely to receive a 3 percent dearness allowance (DA) hike, taking DA from 60 percent to 63 percent . A 4 percent hike appears unlikely unless June's AICPI-IW reading jumps to around 154.5, with the final outcome depending on June inflation data and the government decision. Performance-linked mutual fund fee versus TER: How SEBI's new model works and why fund houses are hesitant. SEBI has proposed a performance-based fee framework for mutual funds, linking fees to a fund's performance rather than a fixed ratio. However, industry hesitance stems from operational challenges and complexities associated with variable fee structures. Microsoft, Nvidia, Apple to Amazon - lose $2.3 trillion in June: Is it time to trim your US tech stocks?. The Magnificent Seven tech stocks lost $2.3 trillion in value in June due to uncertainty over AI investments. Major declines were noted for Microsoft and Nvidia. Investors are advised to diversify portfolios and consider market concentration to mitigate risks amidst fluctuating valuations. Filing ITR? Here's what AIS and Form 26AS tell the income tax department about you. As taxpayers gear up for ITR filing in AY 2026-27, understanding the consolidated Form 168 (formerly AIS and Form 26AS) is crucial. This unified document offers a comprehensive view of your financial footprint, detailing income sources, investments, and tax credits. Reviewing it meticulously against your records helps ensure accurate reporting, prevent mismatches, and avoid potential tax notices, streamlining your return submission. ITR filing 2026: 6 costly mistakes salaried taxpayers should avoid before 31 July deadline. As the last date of filing ITR looms closer, it is important to be careful about certain key factors. These include choosing the right ITR form and regime, and cross-verifying taxable income against the disclosures made in AIS/TIS. EPFO portal downtime extended for a day: When will EPF passbook, claims and UAN services be restored?. EPFO services, including online claim submissions and passbook downloads, will be unavailable until July 1, 2026, due to a system migration. Members can check their EPF balance via a missed call if their UAN is linked with Aadhaar, PAN, and bank details. New versus old regime: Earning rupees 20 LPA? Here's which regime saves you over rupees 1 lakh. The tax regime choice becomes more nuanced at a rupees 20 lakh annual salary, where the Section 87A rebate is no longer available. A comparison shows whether common deductions under the old regime can outweigh the new regime's lower tax rates. NRI selling investments? Here's how you can save capital gains tax. Section 215 of the Income Tax Act, 2025 allows eligible NRIs to claim exemption from long-term capital gains tax. But the benefit is available only if strict conditions on investment source and reinvestment are met. Low-volatility mutual funds: Should you invest? Here's what experts say. Low-volatility mutual funds seek to cushion market downturns while keeping investors invested in equities. Understand how the strategy works and who should consider it. From ITR to Aadhaar: Key financial changes kicking in from July 1. Several financial changes come into effect from 1 July, including revised ITR filing deadlines, free Aadhaar email updates and new RBI rules against bank mis-selling. Here's how they could affect you. Modern borrower expects instant payments, real time updates and more. The once-difficult access to information has made borrowers more aware. They compare loan products and interest rates, and regularly track their credit scores. Borrowers now understand the importance of a credit score, and are increasingly conscious of maintaining healthy credit profiles. SBI employee gets relief in harassment case as Bombay HC rules shared auto commute falls outside POSH law's workplace definition. The Bombay High Court has overturned disciplinary action against an SBI employee, ruling that a shared auto-rickshaw is not a 'workplace' under the POSH Act. The employee faced harassment charges after an incident during his commute. The court emphasized that for the Act to apply, transportation must be employer-provided. This decision leaves the merits of the harassment allegation open for other legal avenues. Starting SIP at 25 versus 35: How a 10-year delay can shrink your retirement corpus by nearly rupees 25 lakh. Delaying a SIP by 10 years can significantly reduce retirement corpus due to the loss of compounding. A rupees 1,000 monthly SIP started at 25 years of age grows to an estimated rupees 37.97 lakh by age 60, compared with rupees 13.27 lakh if started at 35 years, assuming a 10 percent annual return.

transcript.title: Science and technology
transcript.text: Next, science and technology. WhatsApp usernames could open the platform to impersonations and scams, warn cybersecurity experts: Here is how. Meta's WhatsApp is launching usernames for privacy, enabling users to interact without disclosing phone numbers. While this can protect user identity, experts warn of risks like impersonation and scams, highlighting the necessity for robust anti-abuse measures to ensure safety amid these changes. Key details about iPhone 18 Pro leak online after hackers gain access to 630 GB data from Tata Electronics. Leaks from Tata Electronics reveal details about Apple's upcoming iPhone 18 Pro, including component suppliers and internal documents after a ransomware attack. This breach, involving over 200,000 files, threatens Apple's supplier relationships and business strategy ahead of the September launch. Samsung Galaxy A27 5G debuts in India with 120Hz AMOLED display, price starts at rupees 28,999. Samsung Galaxy A27 5G features a 6.7-inch Full HD+ Super AMOLED display, Snapdragon 6 Gen 3 chipset, 50MP triple rear camera, and 5,000mAh battery. OnePlus N6 5G launched in India with 8,000mAh battery, Dimensity 6360 Apex processor: Check price and specs. The OnePlus N6 features a 6.75-inch HD display, MediaTek Dimensity 6360 Apex processor, 8,000mAh battery, and military-grade durability. WhatsApp to let users reserve usernames from next week: Here's how it works. WhatsApp introduces a username system to eliminate the need to share your phone number. The company will allow users to select their usernames starting next week. WhatsApp to let users chat without sharing phone numbers: Username feature coming soon. WhatsApp, which says it has more than 3 billion users globally, has until now allowed users to be contacted by anyone who has their phone number. Will iPhone 17 Pro Max cost nearly rupees 2 lakh in India? Leak suggests so. The iPhone 17 Pro, currently priced at rupees 1,34,900, could reportedly retail between rupees 1,54,900 and rupees 1,69,900. The iPhone 17 Pro Max may be priced between rupees 1,84,900 and rupees 1,99,900, compared with its current price of rupees 1,49,900. Samsung Galaxy M47 5G launched in India with 6,000mAh battery, 50MP OIS camera. Samsung has launched the Galaxy M47 5G in India with a price of rupees 22,999. The phone features a Snapdragon 6 Gen 3 chipset, 6.7-inch AMOLED display, 6,000mAh battery, and a 50MP camera. Apple prepares smart glasses to take on Meta, launch tipped for next year. Apple is eyeing the smart glasses market, potentially releasing its first model next year. Apple could be looking to leverage its strong ecosystem to bring in buyers, a report claims iPhone 18 Pro launch date, expected price, processor and camera upgrades. Leaks suggest Apple's iPhone 18 Pro lineup may launch on September 8 or 9. The phone is expected to see a price of around $1,200-$1,300 iPhone 18 Pro, iPhone 18 Pro Max could see up to $200 price hike, says report. Apple could raise the prices for the upcoming iPhone 18 Pro models by up to $200. The iPhone 18 Pro could be priced at $1,200-$1,300, if the new report is to be believed Mint Explainer: Why quantum computing is the next geopolitical race. With multi-billion dollar investments and new US executive orders targeting a 2028 breakthrough, global powers are rushing to secure hardware superiority before 'Q-Day' upends modern encryption. Flipkart GOAT Sale: iPhone 17 gets rupees 12,000 discount, Pro models cheaper by up to rupees 22,000. Flipkart's GOAT Sale is beginning from July 4 with discounts on various products including the iPhone 17 series. The iPhone 17 will be priced at rupees 70,900, the iPhone 17 Pro at rupees 1,12,900, and the iPhone 17 Pro Max at rupees 1,27,900 Google limits Meta's use of its Gemini AI models: Report. Google has limited Meta's access to its Gemini AI models due to high demand. This has disrupted Meta's internal AI projects and affected other Google clients. Despite growing Google Cloud revenue, computing power constraints hindered further growth in the sector. Fake GTA 6 early access websites are stealing gamers' money: Here's how the scam works. Cybersecurity researchers warn that scammers are exploiting GTA 6 hype by offering fake early access for cryptocurrency payments. Soaring iPad, Xbox prices reveal pain of memory chip mess. Apple and Microsoft increased prices for Xbox consoles, Macs, and iPads due to a memory chip shortage driven by AI demand. Moto Pad 70 Pro launched in India with 13-inch 3.5K display, AI features and 10,200mAh battery. The Moto Pad 70 Pro features a 13-inch 3.5K display, Snapdragon 8s Gen 4 processor, and quad speaker setup. It supports 2TB expandable storage and runs on Android 16 OpenAI names Prabhjeet Singh India MD, launches GPT-5.6 Sol with stronger cyber protections. OpenAI has appointed Prabhjeet Singh as Managing Director for India. The company has also announced a limited release of its GPT-5.6 model, which features three variants: Sol, Terra, and Tuna iPhone 18 to get a big RAM upgrade as Apple doubles down on AI: Report. Apple is set to launch the iPhone 18 and iPhone 18e next spring with 9GB and the A20 chipset. The iPhone 18 lineup could also include improved AI features and brighter display, and better optics. India's robotics funding doubles in 2026 first-half, but scale seems far away. India's robotics sector continues to lag its global peers. In 2025, Indian robotics startups raised less than 1 percent of the capital secured by US robotics startups and about 2 percent of that raised in China, according to Tracxn. iPhone Ultra launch back on track after Apple fixes critical foldable issue: Report. Apple's foldable iPhone is set for a September launch after resolving hinge issues, as per a new report Apple price hike: Check updated prices of MacBook Neo, Mac mini M4, iPads and HomePod. Apple has raised prices for its MacBooks, iPads, and other products in India due to increasing memory chip costs. Apple raises MacBook and iPad prices amid memory chip crunch , Check new prices here. Memory chip makers such as Micron have, in recent months, prioritised orders from AI chipmakers like Nvidia, helping them earn record profits but leaving little supply for electronics makers that have been forced to increase prices. Google alerted users seconds before Venezuela's earthquake. Here's how Android phones detect tremors. As two powerful earthquakes struck Venezuela within minutes of each other, several Android users reported receiving Google earthquake alerts before the tremors reached them. The warnings were made possible by Google's Android Earthquake Alerts System.

transcript.title: Development and policy
transcript.text: Now to development, infrastructure and government policy. India Should Stop Panicking About Trump. India's Bengal drops eggs from school lunches: Why that's stoking debate. Why Delhi's New EV Policy Aims Two And Three Wheelers. PBS News: Social media platforms spread hate music in India despite policy violations, new report says. Chartered Accountants play an indispensable role in strengthening India's economy: VP Radhakrishnan. Reforming Fire Safety in India: An Urgent Policy Imperative. India data centre opportunity - From emerging demand hub to integrated data centre powerhouse. MSC deepens India bet with $1.4B Vizhinjam port deal. Studying in India as a SAARC Student: What No One Tells You Before You Apply. Datanomics: India's protein intake still comes largely from cereals.

transcript.title: Across India
transcript.text: Turning to the big national stories. 100+ prominent India, Pakistan voices appeal for 'confidence building measures'. Over 100 prominent Indian and Pakistani citizens have penned an open letter to Prime Ministers Narendra Modi and Shehbaz Sharif, urging them to restore peace and dialogue. The signatories propose confidence-building measures like reinstating full diplomatic ties and resuming normal visa services. They emphasize that continued hostility hinders the prosperity and secure future of millions of young people in both nations, stressing the need for cooperation over confrontation. India, EU advance cooperation on sustainable ship recycling; 3 Indian yards ready for EU recognition. India and the European Union have reaffirmed their commitment to strengthening cooperation in sustainable ship recycling, with both sides expressing confidence over the progress made in facilitating the inclusion of Indian ship recycling facilities under the European Union Ship Recycling Regulation (EUSRR). EU, India have adjusted to new global order: EU Ambassador Herve Delphin. The European Union and India have adjusted and recalibrated their approach to a rapidly evolving global order shaped by multiple geopolitical and economic shocks, Ambassador of the Delegation of the European Union to India Herve Delphin said on Wednesday, as he highlighted the concept of strategic autonomy amid growing global uncertainties. Beyond the sky: India's Gagan now finds uses in other fields like marine navigation. India has achieved a significant milestone with its Gagan satellite navigation system, becoming the first to be certified for the equatorial region. This advanced system, operational since 2015, enhances GPS accuracy and reliability, bolstering aviation safety and finding crucial applications in maritime navigation, telecommunications, and disaster management, reinforcing India's technological self-reliance. 'Targeted act of vandalism': India condemns reported demolition of 125-year-old gurdwara in Pakistan. India has strongly condemned the reported demolition of a 125-year-old Sikh Gurdwara in Pakistan's Farooqabad, terming it a "targeted act of vandalism." The Ministry of External Affairs expressed grave concern over the lack of action by local authorities and the Evacuee Trust Property Board. This incident is seen as part of a continuing pattern of attacks on religious minorities and their places of worship in Pakistan. 'You may try to crush BJP, but people have crush on BJP': Party to Telangana CM Revanth Reddy. NEW DELHI: BJP on Wednesday launched a sharp attack on Telangana chief minister A Revanth Reddy over his remarks against the party, with BJP national spokesperson Sambit Patra saying, "you may try your best to crush the Bharatiya Janata Party, but the people of India have a crush on the party." Committee on demographic changes finalises questionnaire for states and UTs ahead of field visits. NEW DELHI: The high-level committee tasked with studying demographic changes in the country, including those triggered by illegal immigration, informed home minister Amit Shah on Wednesday that it had finalised a detailed questionnaire to seek relevant information from the states and Union territories ahead of field visits, so as to make its on-the-spot analyses more meaningful and interactive. In peak wedding season, mutton goes off Kashmir's wazwan menu. The levy, which traders claim began last year, has meant they have to shell out up to rupees 25,000 per truck at the border. For 3 years, cops 'inflated' salaries. Then AI spotted it. Three police constables from Chhattisgarh's Bastar were arrested this week after government auditors used Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools to uncover an alleged salary fraud. Rajasthan CID arrests Maharashtra man for 'funding ISI-linked espionage network'. Additional Director General of Police (Intelligence) Prafulla Kumar said that a "significant network" of anti-national activities has been uncovered through the arrest of 41-year-old Rafiq Chand Sheikh, who operated a funding network for the ISI. India asks Pakistan to expedite release, repatriation of 188 fishermen, prisoners. Shares list of 386 prisoners and 53 fishermen, believed to be Pakistani nationals, in its custody. Major changes in military leadership with new Army Chief, Vice-Chiefs, CISC and Army Commanders. General Dhiraj Seth took over as the 31st Chief of Army Staff on Wednesday. 'What changed in the last six months', BJP asks Mann as Punjab implements rural job scheme. Punjab's debt-to-GSDP ratio, too, he said, was around 46 percent compared to the national average of 29 percent , and likely the second-highest in the country. General Dhiraj Seth takes over as Army Chief, unveils 'VIJAY' roadmap. 'V' stands for Vigilance along borders, 'I' for Innovation and Transformation, 'J' for Jointness and Integration, 'A' for Atmanirbharta, and 'Y' for Yodha First, Gen Seth said, adding that from an Agniveer to the senior-most veteran, each army personnel is a yodha (fighter)

transcript.title: Around the world
transcript.text: And finally, here is what is happening around the world. Ukrainian charged in Germany over Nord Stream blasts. Ukraine denies involvement in the case which may have serious implications for its relationship with Germany. Nigeria to seek compensation for property abandoned by citizens fleeing South Africa. Nigeria's High Commissioner says businesses and properties left behind by returnees are being documented. Syria's president names final 70 lawmakers to new post-Assad parliament. Fifteen women are appointed, after only six female candidates were selected by regional electoral colleges last October.

transcript.title: Sign-off
transcript.text: That is your briefing. Stay sharp, and have a strong day.
